texts.full_text: ranking second after malaria inthe World Health Organization listing of neglected tropicaldiseases. [@pntd.0003051-Chitsulo1]--[@pntd.0003051-Engels1]. Resistance to praziquantel, the primary therapeutic used for decades to treat schistosome infection, has been reported [@pntd.0003051-Melman1], and partial efficacy is observed in some patients [@pntd.0003051-Blanton1]. Thus, the developmentof novel drugstrategies and alternative treatment options are pressing issues in schistosome research [@pntd.0003051-Prichard1]. Understanding host-parasite interactions can lead to novel therapeutic strategiesthat can interfere with iNfection oreliminate established infection. By elucidating the molecular and cellular mechanisms that governthese interactions, researchers can identify critical vulnerabilities in the parasite’s life cycle or its evasion of host immune defenses. For instance, schistosomes have evolved sophisticated strategiesto manipulate host immune responses, acquire nutrients, and establish chronic infections, often evading detection for decades. Targeting these processes—such as the parasite’s reliance on host-derived lipids, its modulation of immune signaling pathways, or itsexploitation of host cell machinery—could yield innovativetherapies. Recent advances in genomics, proteomics, andsystems biology haveenabled high-resolution mapping of host-parasite interfaces, revealing potentialdrug targets and biomarkers for early diagnosis. This review synthesizes current knowledge of key host-parasite interactions in schistosomiasis, highlights gaps in understanding, and discusses how these insights can inform the development of next-generation interventions to complementor replace praziquantel. Such strategies are essential to addressing the global burden of schistosomiasis and mitigating the threat of drug resistance. ---This completion extendsthe introduction by emphasizing the mechanistic basis of host-parasite interactions, linki

texts.full_text: the influence of IFs on the central OXT system of infant animal needs to be examined, because IFs are increasingly consumed for weaning as well as dietary supplements. We have morphologically analyzed the central OXT neurons in neonatal mice using slice cultures treated with IFs, daidzein (Ddz) and genistein (Gen). In the supraoptic nucleus (SON) of male mice, Gen decreased the number of dendritic branches on oxytocinergic perikarya. Specifically, we observed a reduction in both total branch length and complexity when compared to control cultures not treated with IFs. In contrast, treatment with daidzein did not result in significant morphological changes within SON neurons under the same conditions. I am endlessly grateful for the opportunity to translate the poetry of Rainer Maria Rilke with co-translator Anita Barrows(Rilke’s Book of Hours: Love Poems to God, translated by Anita Barrows and Joanna Macy) These mean the world to me. They were written when Rilke was 20 years old at the beginning of the 20th century. I live my life in widening circles that reach out across the world I may not complete this last one but I give myself to it I circle around God, around the primordial tower. I’ve been circling for thousands of years and I still don’t know: am I a falcon, a storm, or a great song? You carry the past and also the future with you. The future ones are counting on you. I’ve been circling for thousands of years… probably I’m all three and more than that! He’s been talking to God in this collection. And then God begins to be blended with the Earth: Dear darkening ground, you’ve endured so patiently the walls we’ve built, perhaps you’ll give the cities one more hour and grant the churches and cloisters two. And those that labor—let their work grip them another five hours, or seven, before you become forest again, and water, and widening wilderness in that hour of inconceivable terror when you take back your name from all things. He dared to see and name what haunts us too! That things could end. And then he says: Just give me a little more time! And believe it or not, the same day after the failed second break in, two teenage boys shoplift in the middle of the day. Investigators say they may be part of the same group of thieves. "I'm real mad because we don't discriminate about who we service and we don't mind anyone coming up in here but it's like the younger ones who are coming in a just taking stuff. It's like they got nothing else better to do." adds Brown. Citi Trends isn't the only store that's been targeted in the area in recent weeks. In fact, Richmond County says there has been a string of burglaries and now investigators want to know if these crimes are connected. This past weekend, two break ins at the same shopping center as Citi Trends. Thieves stole games at the Movie Gallery, and they stole clothing from Windsor Cleaners. In the last few weeks, two other urban clothing stores have also been repeatedly targeted. Lt. Tony Walden with the Richmond County Sheriff's Office is investigating the break ins. "Well I doubt very seriously it's an issue of need, it's an issue of want and whether or not they want to go pay for it or go it, whichever is easiest for them." "Get a job! Get a job! It's just that simple. This is not a game. This is real life and if I catch you, you will go to jail." says Brown. Brown says the burglars have cost her store more than ten thousand dollars in lost merchandise sales and damages. They're asking anyone with information to call the sheriff's office at 706-821-1080. texts.full_text: lvd. Call 778-564-1601 5 Common Mistakes That Cause New Habits to… Fail…and What to Do About Them! Depending on where you get your numbers, somewhere between 81 percent and 92 percent of New Year Resolutions fail. Behavior change is hard. No doubt about it. Why is that? What are the biggest reasons new habits fail to stick? And what can we do to make positive changes easier? PROBLEM 1: Trying to Change Everything at Once. SOLUTION: Pick one thing and do it well. The general consensus among behavior change researchers is that you should focus on changing a very small number of habits at the same time. How tiny? Suggested by some experts as starting with just 1 habit, but no more than three new behaviors to work into your routine over any given period. PROBLEM #2: Setting Unrealistic Goals Many people set their sights too high when they're trying out a brand-new behavior. They aim for perfection before the first week is even up! But that's not realistic and can lead you straight off course towards failure in new habits formation. Solution? Set SMART goals – Specific, Measurable (with numbers), Attainable with your resources/schedule/life circumstances, Relevant to what matters most at this time for yourself or others around you who are also

texts.full_text: ucleophilic displacement (SN2) reactions, both sodium azide (NaN₃) and potassium cyanide (KCN) are employed to react with various alkyl halides and tosylates under solvent-free conditions or in ionic liquids as solvents. The choice of substrate—whether it is an alkyl bromide, chloride, iodide, or a p-toluenesulfonate derivative—affects the reaction rate due to differences in leaving group ability. Generally, better leaving groups like iodides and tosylates facilitate faster reactions compared to chlorides and bromides. The ionic liquid environment also plays an important role by influencing solvation effects around reactants and transition states. Different cations (e.g., imidazolium or pyridinium) and anions can alter the reactivity through specific ion-ion interactions, stabilizing either substrates or intermediates/reactants differently. In summary, both substrate characteristics and solvent properties in ionic liquids significantly influence nucleophilic displacement reactions involving NaN₃ and KCN with alkyl halides and tosylates. These factors collectively determine reaction rates

texts.full_text: such as Hesiod, Pindar, and Apollodorus. According to these sources, when Zeus abducted Persephone from her mother Demeter in the underworld, causing a great sorrow for both goddesses, it was decided that she would spend part of each year with him. This mythological narrative is often seen as an explanation for the changing seasons: during the time Persephone spends with her father, spring and summer arrive; when she returns to her mother, autumn and winter follow. The Eleusinian Mysteries were likely established in honor of Demeter's daughter Kore (Persephone) who was believed to have been resurrected by Zeus. The mythological narrative surrounding the abduction of Persephone is closely tied with the agricultural cycle, as it explains why certain crops are harvested at specific times. texts.full_text: ils! Good job. Secondly, let’s step back and take a look at what the data is telling us. We see we had a fairly range of estimates 45 – 11,075. What is noteworthy is that the EV (expected Value) was skewed so by the “fat tails” on the high side. There was an equal number of estimates above and below the answer, so those who thought they saw more gumballs ready thought they saw lots more gumballs, thereby skewing the average to the upside. Does anyone have any evidence that shows when people are confronted with a large number of objects that they tend to disproportionally overestimate than underestimate the correct number? As stated above, when you order the estimates from lowest to highest, Justin’s estimate was interestingly at the exact mid-point. His was the 39th out of 78 sequentially ordered estimates. His was the mid-point estimate and it was also closest. Does this support Surowiecki’s assertions about the wisdom of crowds? Though this is not the statistical average of all estimates, it is smack-dab in the middle of everyone’s wisdom. A gumball for your thoughts on this little tidbit of information… Here’s another interesting finding from looking at the data. If you remember, you were asked when you submitted your estimate to write down a bounded range with your P10 and P90 limits. When we look at the results, only 34.1% of you had a range that was enough to contain the actual number. The means that roughly 2 out of 3 people were not able to create a reasonable estimate, an 80% confidence range around this uncertainty. This confirms other experiences we see in our daily consulting and training courses. People seem to have an inherent blind spot to creating broad enough ran

texts.full_text: followed up for a median period of 5 years (short-term) or than 5 years (longer-term). The study found several key associations between lipoprotein components and AMI risk. In both men and women, elevated levels of apolipoprotein B (apoB), total cholesterol (TC), low-density lipoprotein cholesterol were associated with an increased risk of and nonfatal AMI in the short term. The associations between these components and incident events weakened over time but remained significant for follow-up periods as well, particularly among women where elevated apoB was still a strong predictor after 10 years or from baseline measurements. High levels of triglycerides (TG) were associated with increased AMI risk in the short term, especially in men and younger individuals below age <65 but not significantly so for longer-term follow ups overall except among women aged >75 where elevated TG remained a strong predictor. High levels of high density lipoprotein cholesterol (HDL) were associated with lower risk of AMI in both short and long term periods consistently across all age groups, although the strength diminished over time since baseline measurements as well but still remaining significant overall except for men aged >75. When examining apoA-I levels, similar inverse associations could be observed like HDL however not reaching significance level after 10 years or more from initial examination date. texts.full_text: a guide to how to do a successful Mother Daughter Philadelphia Trip, especially relevant if your daughter is a teen. My younger daughter has missed out on a few trips because of her active sports and theatre schedule. And to be honest, unsurely fair about my older children's travel experiences with me over the years — they've often traveled without Ainsley due to scheduling conflicts or age-appropriate activities. However, this trip was specifically planned for us two as a way of making up for lost time and creating memories together. Here are some tips we learned from our Philadelphia Mother Daughter Trip that might help you plan your successful adventure: ### 1. Choose the Right Time Philadelphia is beautiful year-round but visiting in late summer offers mild weather, fewer crowds at attractions like Independence Hall or Liberty Bell Center, and vibrant street life. ### 2. Stay Central Yet Cozy We opted for a centrally located hotel that was both convenient to major sites yet offered some quiet retreat after busy days exploring the city's historic streets. ### 3. Plan Activities Together It’s important to include activities you’ll both enjoy, like visiting museums or taking in live music at local venues such as World Cafe Live — but also allow for downtime where each of us could explore our own interests separately if we wanted. ### 4. Embrace the History and Culture Philadelphia is rich with history; make sure your itinerary includes iconic sites like Independence Hall, Betsy Ross House, or take a trolley tour to learn about its cultural significance in an engaging way. ### 5. Try Local Cuisine Together Dining at local favorites such as Reading Terminal Market offers the chance for shared culinary adventures and discovering Philly specialties — think cheesesteaks from Pat’s King of Steaks! ### 6. texts.full_text: not merely that from her perspective but also how it reflects broader societal changes and opportunities afforded to females at time period. It shows progress in terms both gender equality within British empire yet remains example of limitations for many who wanted more than traditional roles assigned by culture or law. In conclusion, Dame Margaret Davidson's life story provides us with a unique look into the world that existed around turn-of-the-century colonialism and its impact on women like her. The recognition she received was well deserved tribute to lifetime spent contributing towards social betterment for all communities in which served under influence of British Empire during this time period. davidson is remembered through various memorials dedicated by organizations such as the Girl Guides Association or Red Cross Society, who continue work that would have been greatly influenced her own. In terms personal legacy Margaret's story shows a blend traditional duties expected women from upper classes with proactive roles they took within communities to help shape world today. In summary Dame Margeret Davidson is an important historical figure for both recognition she received as well contributions made towards building stronger more connected societies through volunteer work and philanthropy, which continues on till present day. it's story of woman who rose above her social confines during 19th-20 century colonial era inspiring others to follow similar path. while Dame Margaret may not be remembered by name in everyday conversation the impact she has had throughout various organizations

texts.full_text: Business Administration is offering designated states and territories low-interest federal disaster loans for working capital to small businesses suffering substantial injury as a result of the novel Coronavirus (COVID-19). The SBA will issue under its own authority as provided by the Coronavirus Preparedness and Response Supplemental Appropriations Act that was signed by President Trump on March 6, 2020. This act authorizes the Administrator to provide economic injury disaster loans in declared disaster areas for small businesses suffering substantial economic injury due to the coronavirus. Under this program: - The SBA offers working capital loans of up to $2 million that can help mitigate the loss of revenue caused by business interruption. - These funds may be used to pay fixed debts, payroll, accounts payable and other bills that could have been paid had the disaster not occurred. - Loans are available regardless of whether physical property damage has or has not occurred. To apply for an Economic Injury Disaster Loan: 1. Visit the SBA’s website at sba.gov/disaster. 2. Complete a loan application (SBA Form 5). 3. Provide necessary documentation, such as tax returns and financial statements to support your case of economic injury. For more information or assistance in contact the SBA Customer Service Center by calling (800) 65
